complexity.justification: Single-file sequential read-and-display loop with no output file, no transformation logic, and one error-handling block

annotation_metadata.note: Every 'lines' range and 'source_snippet' below was checked line-by-line against the uploaded VSCBEX02.cbl using cat -n; paragraph names are the real COBOL paragraph names (e.g. '1000-Begin-Job'), not invented labels.

structures.name: PROGRAM_METADATA
structures.source_snippet:
* Program name:    VSCBEX02
* Original author: David Stagowski
* Description: Example 02: Indexed File Processing: Read All
structures.confidence_justification: Comment header block is visually delimited by asterisk banners at lines 1 and 21
structures.description: Program header comment block describing purpose, author, and maintenance log

structures.name: IDENTIFICATION
structures.confidence_justification: IDENTIFICATION DIVISION and PROGRAM-ID are explicit COBOL reserved-word headers

structures.name: ENVIRONMENT_DIV
structures.source_snippet:
SELECT INFile
ASSIGN TO "../idata/customer.idat"
ORGANIZATION IS INDEXED
RECORD KEY IS INFile-Cust-ID
ACCESS MODE IS SEQUENTIAL
FILE STATUS IS WS-INFile-Status.
structures.confidence_justification: ENVIRONMENT DIVISION / FILE-CONTROL / SELECT are explicit; single file assignment with no ambiguity
structures.description: Declares INFile as an indexed customer file opened for sequential access

structures.name: DATA_DIV
structures.source_snippet:
DATA DIVISION.
FILE SECTION.
FD  INFile.
    COPY CUSTOMER REPLACING ==:tag:== BY ==INFile==.
structures.confidence_justification: DATA DIVISION, FILE SECTION, and WORKING-STORAGE SECTION headers are explicit
structures.description: File record layout (via COPY CUSTOMER), file-status working-storage, a single read counter, and the end-of-job message

structures.name: MAINLINE
structures.source_snippet:
PROCEDURE DIVISION.
0000-Mainline.
    PERFORM 1000-Begin-Job.
    PERFORM 2000-Process UNTIL WS-INFile-EOF.
    PERFORM 3000-End-Job.
    GOBACK.
structures.confidence_justification: PROCEDURE DIVISION plus the 0000-Mainline paragraph unambiguously mark program entry; the driving loop is an inline PERFORM...UNTIL, not a separate END-PERFORM block
structures.description: Program entry point; drives the read-all loop with a single in-line PERFORM UNTIL WS-INFile-EOF

structures.name: BEGIN_JOB
structures.source_snippet:
1000-Begin-Job.
    OPEN INPUT INFile.
D    DISPLAY "INFile Status: " WS-INFile-Status.
    PERFORM 5000-Read-INFile.
structures.confidence_justification: OPEN and a priming READ (via PERFORM) are direct initialization actions
structures.description: Opens INFile for input and performs the priming read that the sequential PERFORM UNTIL loop relies on

structures.name: PROCESS_RECORD
structures.source_snippet:
2000-Process.
    IF WS-INFile-Good
       DISPLAY INFile-Customer-Record
    END-IF.
    PERFORM 5000-Read-INFile.
structures.confidence_justification: Simple conditional DISPLAY followed by the next read; no branching complexity
structures.description: Displays the current record if the last read was successful, then reads the next record to advance the loop

structures.name: END_JOB
structures.source_snippet:
3000-End-Job.
    DISPLAY EOJ-End-Message.
    DISPLAY "   Records Read: " FD-INFile-Record-Cnt
    CLOSE INFile.
structures.confidence_justification: DISPLAY and CLOSE statements clearly indicate termination/cleanup
structures.description: Displays the end-of-job banner and read count, then closes INFile

structures.name: READ_INFILE
structures.source_snippet:
5000-Read-INFile.
    READ INFile
       AT END SET WS-INFile-EOF TO TRUE
    END-READ.
    IF WS-INFile-Good
       ADD +1 TO FD-INFile-Record-Cnt
    ELSE
       IF WS-INFile-EOF
          NEXT SENTENCE
       ELSE
          DISPLAY "** ERROR **: 5000-Read-INFile"
          ...
          PERFORM 3000-End-Job
          MOVE 8 TO RETURN-CODE
          GOBACK
       END-IF
    END-IF.
structures.confidence_justification: READ/AT END is explicit, but the paragraph combines EOF handling, counter increment, and a hard-error abend path into one logical unit
structures.description: Reads the next indexed record; on EOF sets the loop-exit flag, on genuine I/O error displays diagnostics and abends with RETURN-CODE 8
